title: Crispy Adventurous Cantonese Galangal Bao
description: A crispy, jammy, and adventurous Cantonese dish featuring galangal paste, perfect for a keto-friendly meal. The combination of textures and flavors will leave you wanting more.

ingredients.name: Galangal Paste
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use fresh galangal for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Green Onions
ingredients.notes: Use the white and light green parts for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Lard
ingredients.notes: You can use vegetable oil as a substitute if needed.

ingredients.name: Scallions
ingredients.notes: Use the green parts for garnish.

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, galangal paste, ginger, green onions, salt, sugar, and white pepper. Mix well until all the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Divide the meat mixture into 4 equal parts and shape each part into a ball.
Place a ball of meat into the center of each bao bun and flatten it slightly with your hand.
Brush the tops of the buns with lard and sprinkle with cornstarch.
Cook the buns in the air fryer for 5-6 minutes on each side or until golden brown and crispy.
Garnish with scallions and serve immediately.
Cook the buns in batches if necessary to prevent overcrowding the air fryer.
Make sure to cook the buns until they are crispy and golden brown for the best flavor and texture.
Let the buns rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the filling to set.
You can also cook the buns in a skillet on the stovetop if you prefer.
You can adjust the amount of galangal paste to your liking, but make sure to use fresh galangal for the best flavor.
You can substitute the ground pork with ground beef or ground chicken if you prefer.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
